title: Flu: What To Do If You Get Sick
license_notes: Use as government source support for flu self-care, isolation, high-risk groups, and emergency warning signs. Avoid implying CDC endorses local discharge wording.
supports: People with mild flu illness should usually stay home except to get medical care.
People with emergency warning signs should seek emergency care.
People at higher risk of serious flu complications should contact a health care provider for advice.
Emergency warning signs include trouble breathing, chest pain, severe weakness, dehydration, confusion, seizures, and symptoms that improve then worsen.
